title: relocate /var/lib/containers dir via mount --bind prevents container from start
body:
I've relocated the the containers data dir via linux fs command `mount --bind` in order to change the real location where container data - especially volume data - are stored.

`> mount --bind /srv/containers /var/lib/containers`

This prevents the container from starting with the following error: 

```
root@unhaim ~# podman run -it fedora  bash
bash: error while loading shared libraries: libc.so.6: cannot change memory protections
```
The mounted source dir `/srv/containers` is a local dir owned by root.

* podman version 1.0.0
* as root
* on 
  Operating System: Fedora 29 (Server Edition)
       CPE OS Name: cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:29
            Kernel: Linux 4.20.5-200.fc29.x86_64
      Architecture: x86-64

comments.body: Can you run `podman --log-level=debug run -it fedora bash` and pastebin the result? That would give us a good idea as to where in the init process we're failing.

Could this be SELinux?

What is the permissions on /srv?

ls -lad /srv

```
root@unhaim ~# mount --bind /srv/containers/ /var/lib/containers
```
```
root@unhaim ~# ls -lad /srv
drwxr-xr-x. 7 root root 111 22. Feb 08:03 /srv/
```
```
root@unhaim ~# ls -lad /srv/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 4 root root 34 22. Feb 08:05 /srv/containers/
```

This worked for me:

```
sh-4.4# mkdir -p /srv/containers
sh-4.4# mount -o bind /srv/containers /var/lib/containers
sh-4.4# restorecon -v /var/lib/containers/
Relabeled /var/lib/containers from unconfined_u:object_r:var_t:s0 to unconfined_u:object_r:container_var_lib_t:s0
sh-4.4# podman run -ti fedora ls
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2ec1fbfd44b7: 85.73 MiB / 85.73 MiB [=========================] 15s
Copying config d7372e6c93c6: 1.99 KiB / 1.99 KiB [==========================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
bin  boot  dev	etc  home  lib	lib64  lost+found  media  mnt  opt  proc  root	run  sbin  srv	sys  tmp  usr  var
```

@rhatdan 

Hi, is there any convention on remapping dirs for overlay?
Here is what I did: 
1. do the mounts
> mount /dev/vdb1 /root/mnt
mkdir -p /root/mnt
mount -o bind /root/mnt /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
restorecon  -R -v   /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay


2. check the mounts
after executed the commands above,
there are two mount points for /dev/vdb1 in /proc/mounts

3. do a normal status check
> podman ps 
> podman images

4. recheck the mount points
> cat /proc/mounts

In step 4 I cannot see the vdb1 mount on /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay

Since libpod does not have a daemon to restart to reload the change, is there any conventional way to do this?
